title: Intensely Smoky Canadian Skirt Steak Tagine
description: A smoky and savory Canadian dish featuring skirt steak, perfect for a brunch mood, and diabetic-friendly. This recipe is made in a ceramic tagine vessel.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together smoked paprika, garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the spice mixture all over the sliced skirt steak, making sure to coat evenly.
Heat the olive oil in the ceramic tagine vessel over medium-high heat.
Sear the skirt steak strips until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes, then remove from the tagine and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the tagine and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped dried apricots, chicken broth, and browned skirt steak back to the tagine.
Cover the tagine and transfer to the preheated oven, cooking for 2 hours.
While the tagine is cooking, mix the sodium alginate with water in a small bowl.
Using a syringe or spoon, drop small amounts of the alginate mixture into a bath of cold water to create spheres, then set aside.
After the tagine has finished cooking, remove from the oven and stir in the spherified alginate pearls.
